insights.title: Macro modulation is not sample-accurate and is designed for slow movements only
insights.summary: Christoph Hart explicitly confirmed that macro modulation is not sample-accurate and never will be. It was designed as a quick way to add slow movement to controls (e.g., slow pad filter sweeps, randomized jitter), not as a precision modulation system. For fast or exact modulation, use HISE's built-in modulators instead.

insights.title: Macro modulation works by moving the knob value, not injecting into the modulation chain
insights.summary: The macro modulation system operates at lower resolution than the modulation chain. It effectively moves the target parameter's knob value very fast - this means the parameter's own built-in smoothing is what you hear, not a dedicated modulation signal. Use proper HISE modulators for high-accuracy modulation.

insights.title: Macro Modulator placed in a Container does not receive note-on events
insights.summary: Containers have no voice logic and no MIDI processing, so a Macro Modulator (or any envelope/matrix modulator) placed inside a Container will not restart on note-on. The modulator runs but never resets its phase. Use the modulator inside a sound generator instead.
insights.verify_hint: grep 'handleHiseEvent' in MacroControlModulator.cpp; check container voice logic in ModulatorSynthChain

insights.title: Macro control modulator index is off by one in the UI
insights.summary: David Healey reported that the macro control modulator index shown in the UI is off by one relative to the actual macro slot it controls. Reported in 2023; verify whether this has been fixed in current builds.
insights.verify_hint: grep 'MacroIndex' or 'macroIndex' in MacroControlModulator.cpp; check combo box population in MacroControlModulatorEditorBody

insights.title: Macro system uses a 0-127 range internally; non-standard knob ranges require normalization
insights.summary: David Healey noted that the macro control system operates on a 0-127 range internally, which causes mismatch when controlling knobs with custom ranges. Normalized values (0-1) would make more sense but are not what the system uses.
insights.verify_hint: grep 'macroControllerMoved' in MacroControlModulator.cpp; check clamping and range handling

insights.title: Macro control values saved in presets behave inconsistently
insights.summary: User reports that macro control values do save into presets but the behavior is described as 'wonky'. Treat preset round-tripping of macro modulator state as potentially unreliable until verified.
insights.verify_hint: grep 'restoreFromValueTree' or 'exportAsValueTree' in MacroControlModulator.cpp; check MacroIndex and MacroValue serialization
